skill.name: requesting-code-review
skill.description: Provides a structured workflow for requesting code review from a code-reviewer subagent after development work or before merge.
skill.summary: Request structured code review with clear context, Git range, severity categories, and merge readiness assessment.
skill.author: ZhanlinCui

security_audit.summary: Static external-command findings are confirmed, but the commands are local Git inspection commands used for reviewing a diff. The weak-cryptography and system-reconnaissance findings are false positives from Git SHA terminology and example review text. No evidence found of prompt injection, network access, credential access, data exfiltration, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Local Git Command Examples
low_findings.description: The skill instructs users or agents to run local Git commands such as git rev-parse and git diff. This is expected for a code review workflow and does not show network access or exfiltration, but it should run only in trusted repositories with trusted commit refs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and limited to Git operations. The context supports legitimate review use, with only minor risk from shell execution in untrusted contexts.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Flags Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The high static findings do not identify cryptographic code. They occur in markdown context around Git SHA references, placeholders, and review checklist text rather than hashing or encryption implementation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines found no cryptographic functions, hash computation, or weak algorithm use. The surrounding content is documentation for Git commit ranges and code review.

low_findings.title: System Reconnaissance Flags Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The low static reconnaissance findings appear in review guidance and example output. No commands collect host identity, environment variables, process lists, network configuration, or other system inventory data.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are prose inside a review template or checklist. I found no reconnaissance commands or data collection behavior in those locations.

dangerous_patterns.title: Shell Command Substitution for Git SHAs
dangerous_patterns.description: The documentation shows shell command substitution for deriving BASE_SHA and HEAD_SHA. The commands are benign Git lookups, but agents should avoid substituting untrusted free-form values into shell commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The syntax is clearly present and executable in a shell. The intended values are Git refs, so the concern is contextual rather than evidence of malicious behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Git Diff Command with Placeholder Refs
dangerous_patterns.description: The review template includes git diff commands with BASE_SHA and HEAD_SHA placeholders. This supports the skill purpose, but commit refs should be validated or generated from Git rather than accepted from untrusted text.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicitly present and central to the review workflow. They are local read-only Git commands, so the residual risk is low.

content.value_statement: Development teams need timely review before defects reach main branches. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a repeatable review request format with context, Git range, severity levels, and merge assessment.
actual_capabilities: Defines when to request code review during feature work, refactoring, and pre-merge checks.
Provides Git commands for selecting base and head commits for a review range.
Supplies a code-reviewer prompt template with implementation, requirements, and Git range placeholders.
Structures review output into strengths, critical issues, important issues, minor issues, recommendations, and readiness.
Encourages acting on critical and important findings before continuing development.
limitations: Requires a compatible agent environment that can use or simulate a code-reviewer subagent.
Does not perform review by itself without the project code, requirements, and Git range.
Git command examples assume the working directory is a valid Git repository.
Review quality depends on the accuracy of the implementation summary and requirements supplied by the user.

use_cases.title: Pre-Merge Review
use_cases.description: Ask an AI reviewer to inspect the final diff before merging a feature branch.

use_cases.title: Task-by-Task Plan Execution
use_cases.description: Review each completed task in a larger implementation plan before starting the next task.

use_cases.title: Bug Fix Verification
use_cases.description: Request a focused review after fixing a complex bug to check regressions, tests, and edge cases.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Review Request
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review after a small change.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my latest code change for correctness, tests, and obvious bugs. Use the code-reviewer format and give a merge readiness verdict.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Git Range
prompt_templates.scenario: Standard review with explicit base and head commits.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the changes from BASE_SHA to HEAD_SHA. Compare the diff against the requirements below and categorize issues as Critical, Important, or Minor.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Compliance Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate review after completing a planned task.
prompt_templates.prompt: I completed this task from the implementation plan. Check whether the code meets the listed requirements, identify missing behavior, and assess production readiness.

prompt_templates.title: Release Readiness Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before merge or deployment.
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a production readiness review for this feature. Check architecture, security, migrations, backward compatibility, tests, and documentation. Include specific file and line references.

output_examples.input: A developer asks for review after adding a verification function and related tests.
output: Strengths identify clear architecture, meaningful tests, and useful error handling.
Important issues highlight a missing progress indicator and unclear validation behavior.
Assessment says the change is ready with fixes before merge.

output_examples.input: A technical lead requests review for a Git range before merging a feature branch.
output: The reviewer compares the diff with the stated requirements.
Critical, Important, and Minor sections separate blocking risks from follow-up improvements.
Recommendations explain concrete fixes and the final merge verdict.

best_practices: Provide a concise implementation summary, the requirements, and an exact Git range before requesting review.
Fix critical and important issues before continuing to later tasks or merging code.
Push back on incorrect review comments with code evidence, tests, and technical reasoning.
anti_patterns: Requesting review without the relevant requirements or implementation context.
Treating style comments as blockers while ignoring correctness, security, or data loss risks.
Proceeding after important findings without recording a decision or applying a fix.

faq.question: What does this skill help me do?
faq.answer: It helps you request structured AI code review with context, Git range, issue severity, and merge readiness assessment.

faq.question: Does it run the review automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides the workflow and prompt template for a compatible reviewer agent or subagent.

faq.question: Why does it use Git commands?
faq.answer: The commands identify the base and head commits so the reviewer can inspect the correct diff.

faq.question: Can I use it outside Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The template can guide Claude, Codex, Claude Code, or another AI coding assistant that can review project changes.

faq.question: What should I include in a review request?
faq.answer: Include what changed, the requirements or plan, the base commit, the head commit, and any known constraints.

faq.question: How should I handle reviewer feedback?
faq.answer: Fix critical issues immediately, resolve important issues before proceeding, and record minor issues for later work.
